desc:
The trail bends south-west here. It leads along a northern branch of the Bruinen
towards a mountain range in the north. The ground is still muddy from the ford
in the south and you discover tracks leading north. You can see the remains of
an ancient, burned down guard-post or small fort. Someone must have guarded the
ford against people or armies coming from the south.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail leads east-west here and it leads slowly towards a cliff in the north.
The cliff is pretty high and you feel icy winds coming down from it. The trail
is leading through barren plains and the landscape is dreary. Under the cliff
you will find shelter against the uncomfortable weather.
name: Trail under the Cliffs

desc:
The path bends north-west here. It leads away from the cliff in the south to a
mountain chain in the north. There is snow on top of the mountains. The path is
surrounded by empty plains.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The path bends east-south here. You can discover signs that the path is still
inuse. Tracks of small groups passing swiftly are here. Barren plains surround
the path. The plains stretch north until they reach another cliff.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The path leads east-west here. Every once in a while you notice skeletons,
bleached by the sun, beside the path. It becomes colder and colder and you are
sure that many of the dead travellers were frozen to death.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
The path leads east-west here. Icy winds blow into your face as you walk on
towards a branch of the river Bruinen. Further in the east you see that the path
leads northwards into a long forgotten, evil land. The mountain-range in north
therefore must be the Coldfells.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
This path, now merely a swath of trampled down vegetation, once was an often
used route to the evil northern kingdom of Angmar. Now fallen, Angmar is rarely
visited except by wicked and fell minions of the Enemy. The ground and grass
beneath your feet is laden heavily with frost, numbing your toes as the wind
numbs nearly all of the rest of you. The path continues to the north and west.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
Turning to the east and south, the path to Angmar heads northward, between the
cliffs to the east and the terrain to the west. The chill wind blows strongly
from the North, bringing with it a heavily-laden frost, telling a tale of woe
for the evil of Angmar.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
Following the clear and cold river that laps upon the banks that border the east
of the road, this path bends west and north. Farther to the south, a small patch
of woods is nestled in a small nook between the river and the sheer cliff-face,
refusing either to be swept away by the current or be pushed out by the stone.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
Straight north and south this path goes, directly itself un-erringly to the
north, to the fallen ruins of Angmar. You feet trod on, leaving footprints where
the frozen grass does not straighten. Surely easy to track, but you hope
fervently that nothing would want to follow you.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
The path to Angmar diverges from the cliff-faces to the east, fearing to stray
from the fastest course. Why anything or anybody would want to explore further
northward, you have little idea...only evil could await you there. Pulling your
cloak tighter against the icy winds, you march onward, determined.
name: Path to Angmar

desc:
You are going through an endless looking snow-field. The snow is driven away by
the icy wind out of the north. Soon you tracks are blown away by the wind and
you risk getting lost.
name: Cold Plain

desc:
It becomes colder and colder. Here is much snow that has build dunes and the
snow reaches up to your hip. The high snow makes it possible for you to see your
tracks at least for some minutes before they are covered under the snow again.
name: Icy Plain

desc:
The ground is slightly covered with snow and an ice-cold wind chills your bones.
A few crippled bushes exist here, barely covered with dark-green needles.
name: Plain

desc:
Snow over snow, here is so much snow that it makes you sick. The reflecting
sunlight makes it harder and harder for you to see anything. You should leave
here soon or you will slowly turn blind. To the south-west you can see a forest
but large dunes of snow in front of it make it impossible for you to go there.
name: Icy Plain

desc:
Approaching the mighty joining, the river takes leave of the path and boldly and
confidently flows south, its icy current allowing no argument from any that
travel its course. The cold waters seem to rejoice and splash amongst
themselves; soon they will have less to fear from Angmar.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
Faster and faster the current flows, coming closer and closer to the lands of
the south that are not choked with the dust of ruin and evil. To the east, the
cliff-sides turn from the river, and now broken and rough lands border the path.
An eerie howl gives the impression that the river is not totally safe.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
Running along the little-used trail that goes north to where Angmar once stood,
this branch of the Bruinen seems eager to join with its brothers. Flowing
steadily and strongly to the south, its cold depths occasionally, and very
briefly, reveal some silvery fish.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
The deep waters follow the bend of the path, turning briefly westward before, a
bit away, they turn back to the south. Although the stone cliff to the south
forces the river to abandon the hardy woods to the west, the river does not
leave without battering the wall continuously, and in years to come the current
may know victory. Although rushed to leave the north, it seems to be patiently
trying to destroy the cliff.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
The clear, icy waters flow past the earthy banks of the forest to the east,
granting the life-giving water to the pines that crave it. To the west, you can
see a grassy path, and farther to the east, over the trees, a mighty cliff, red
and brown and yellow, stands over the river and its surroundings.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
The path to Angmar and this river follow each other dedicatedly, perhaps
together they feel safer from the fell fortune of the North. Nevertheless, both
seem to go as fast as they can to leave that accursed realm. The cold current
pushes your southward, eager to continue its journey.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
Directly east of the river stands a cliffside, directing even the strong current
of this eager branch with an indifferent ease. But the red-brown sandy bottom of
the river tell the tale of who really wins in this eternal struggle. To the
south, a slight rising in the ground pushes the river westward, allowing a small
grove of trees to survive.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
Directly east of the river stands a cliffside, directing even the strong current
of this eager branch with an indifferent ease. But the red-brown sandy bottom of
the river tell the tale of who really wins in this eternal struggle. The river
flows to the south, fleeing the cold realm of Angmar and seeking safety with the
other tributaries with which it will join.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
The plain is a continuation of the cliffs east and south. It is covered with
large boulders which make it possible for you to avoid the worst of the cold
blowing wind. In the north part of the plain, near a winding path, you find a
weathered wooden cross.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
The cliff ends here and the surrounding areas all have the same altitude, but
the landscape is still rising towards the north. Brown moss covers the cliff and
little black creatures are running around. The spare vegetation is stooped to
the south from the constant blowing wind.
name: End of the Cliff

desc:
The river bends south-east here. The river is surrounded by high cliffs to the
north-west and south-east. The cliffs channel the water and make the river very
wild for a small section. You can see calmer parts near the riverbanks where you
can progress further.
name: Northern Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
Clear, deep and strong this cold river runs, flowing steadily over any rocks
that may try to obstruct its path. It veers west, avoiding the rough ground of
the steppes to the south. The current comes from the north, pushing to join with
other tributaries to form the mighty Bruinen.
name: Branch of the Bruinen

desc:
The cliff slowly turns northwards. To the east you can see a northern branch of
the Bruinen flowing towards a crossing with another branch of the river. The
cliff builds a canyon with a cliff further east. The trail down the cliff bends
to the south and leads along the Bruinen towards a destination unknown.
name: Edge of the Cliff

desc:
No vegetation and no rocks are here. Just a grey, empty plain. A path is leading
through the plain, you wonder where it leads to. Going near the cliff looks
pretty dangerous. Icy gusts of wind may blow you away and over the cliff.
name: Cliff

desc:
The river bends north-south here. To the east are rocky steppes and to the west
a riverbank that is too high and steep to climb up. However, roughly carved logs
have been haphazardly thrown together to allow passage. The work looks quite
recent. To the north a region of rapids begins and the white caps of fast moving
water can be seen. You see fish jumping out of the water trying to avoid the
rapids.
name: Hastily Built Bridge over a River

desc:
Between the two tributaries, and just west of a cliff, these grounds seem rather
out of place. The going is rough, as the footing is uneven and uncertain; a
hasty step could result in a sprain--the very thought of being stranded here,
unable to leave, makes you shiver...and not from the cold.
name: Steppes

desc:
Closer to the cliffs, the ground gets more broken and rockier, and your legs
weary of their travels, but resting here seems to be the less desirable
alternative. The freezing river is to the east and south, and north the cliffs
begin.
name: Steppes

desc:
The ground rises, approaching the red-orange cliffs that stand to the
north-east. The steep sides of the cliff might provide a windbreak, but surely
any other creatures that frequent this land must know that too. Perhaps
returning to the road would be best.
name: Before a Cliff

desc:
Small and insignificant you appear at the base of these mighty cliffs, rising so
far above you head and cutting through the clouds themselves. The colour of the
rock of the cliff-face, reds and browns and oranges intermixed, remind you of a
sunset, but no sunlight warms this cold land, only the dull-grey of the sky
could provide any illumination.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
The ruddy cliff continues here, blocking any hope of going north. Any fancy to
climb up the face ends quite quickly if the sheer side is gazed at. How could
any puny human dare challenge the immortality of these ancient stone landmarks?
The river, though swift and cold, would be much easier to travel on.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
Still the mountainside continues, and it seems to go on perpetually east and
west. The rocky ground seems to have been, at one point, part of the cliff face;
but over the centuries and ages the wind and rain and ice have pried from the
wall any rock even slightly loose. You cover you head quickly, fearful of any
latecoming rocks that may decide to join their brethen.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
A hard ride either west or east would not bring you to the end of this
cliff-face, so wide and broad is it. Its rich red-earth tones are only
occasionally marred by the nests of various bird of prey that dot the rock with
darker patches of brown and black. You wonder how a raptor could survive in this
land, barren and lifeless, devoid of almost any prey, and living in a nest
continually battered by the icy winds that blast the mountainside. They must be
truly hardy beasts.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
As your legs grow weary of this rocky base, your eyes travels over to the
southwest, where the river flows by at a brisk pace. You can see no feasible way
to enter it, and content yourself with knowing that it is not that far off. This
must truly be a savage place, you think to yourself, if the freezing waters of
the tributary seem mild by comparison.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
Stoney silence is all that you hear here, except for your own breathing and
footsteps. Rarely, a piercing cry of a raptor is heard, shattering the silence
with a chilling hunting cry. You pull your cloak closer to your body, and hurry
on in your journey.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
Curses escape from your lips as these cliffs continue, and show no sign of
ending anywhere soon. Your legs ask for a respite from the futile task of
following the cliffs to their end, and your skin begs for warmth and shelter
from the icy winds.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
The cliffs continue on, racing alongside the river and forcing any who pass to
follow its course. However, the ground here seems to be made of smaller pieces
of rock, allowing your legs a momentary rest before starting off again onto
rougher terrains. The cliff is a majestic red-brown colour, and it almost seems
warm in this cold clime.
name: Base of a Cliff

desc:
The cliffs continue along the river to the south. Erosion has eaten several feet
from the outside of the road, but the grey rock has otherwise withstood the
elements. Gazing south, a forest of pine stands haughtily. The Grey Mountains
surround the pines almost engulfing them.
name: A Path by the River

desc:
Turning east with the river, the russet colour of the cliffs begins to be
corrupted by the drab greys and blacks of harder stone. The rocky ground that
forms the bank of the river has been worn away with time so that the river is an
immediate drop from the edge of the path. Very little vegetation survives here,
but what there is looks quite hardy. A bit north of the immediate cliffs, mighty
mountains rise up, the first signs of Hithaeglir.
name: A Path by the River

desc:
The soft whitewash from a single birds nest stands in glaring contrast to the
grey slate that dominates the landscape. The path firms up here and shows signs
of recent use and continues on to the east and west.
name: Path at the Base of a Cliff

desc:
The path widens out to a road here. Grey rock again forming the bed of the road.
The ground swells upward slightly and continues in a definite upward grade to
the east. The river dwindles into the distance and breaks away to the east. The
summits of the stately cliffs still tower high above.
name: A Road by the River

desc:
Steeper and steeper the incline becomes to the east. The road, while easier
travelling than the path is definitely more taxing on the body. All the land
seem grey now, barren and lifeless up to the whitecaps of the mountains. No
natural animals can be found here, no matter how thoroughly you look. There
doesn't appear to be any other way into the mountains but this way.
name: A Path up an Incline

desc:
The cliffs abruptly end as the river veers away for a time. The road winds its
way slowly up a hill before heading west in the distance. The sinewy path of the
road can be seen snaking in and out of jagged outcroppings of grey rock. The
only way is up or back west.
name: End of the Cliff

desc:
The road crests here and begins a slow jaunt to the north. The cliffs are no
more. They have been engulphed by the dreadful mountains that rule this region,
which seems to lie pale and weak below these terrible heights. One such peak you
stand on now, yet instead of feeling mighty and above the world, you feel all,
alone, and utterly helpless. With a simple shrug these mountains could end your
existence -- but they deign not to notice your plight.
name: A Road up a Mountain

desc:
All about you is mountains. The scrubby fauna doesn't seem to be able to survive
at this altitude. The road is little more than broken stubble here, a grey steep
slope that helps your legs little. The trail of rubble bends to the east again.
name: A Bend in the Road

desc:
More and more trail lies in either direction. All there is, is the trail.
Impassable badlands border the road on each side, and it is all you can do to
cling to its rocky surface, the only remnant of civilisation for miles. And it
climbs, higher and higher, to the east. Far in the distance the Misty Mountains
rise before you threateningly.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The road is hard and stony under your feet, and the sharp and bony rocks seem to
penetrate hard leather boots. Slowly the path rises to the east, in a roundabout
way. There are many obstacles to pass; many fallen trees to circumvent,
splintering boulders to clamber over, and crumbling ledges to avoid. All around
the land is wasted and dark, like an insiduous graying has overtaken it, sucking
the life and vitality, and leaving only a pale carcass of shale and carbon.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The trail has ascended enough to form a slight lookout. Peering around, you can
see the pine forest falling away to the southwest, and in its place barren and
broken badlands spread across the steppes of the Hithaeglir. To the north,
across a frigid shining river, the vanguard of the Misty Mountains rises up grey
and solemn, subjugating the majestic cliffs far to the west.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Your wearying legs definitely detect an upward grade in this trail. Slowly it
will climb up, ascending up the mountains to roll down to the forests below, on
the other side. The miles between you and the path's completion are too numerous
to even comprehend, many a day will you be travelling before you reach its end.
A frantic thought enters your mind : IF you reach the end. You hear the crawling
vermin that inhabit the Misty Mountains do not take kindly to travellers.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Turning east and south, this trail has fully diverged from the forest. Now,
however, the grey and drab landscape that slowly surrounds you seems to draw
your life and vitality from your body. The dark and frightening forest seemed so
alive and welcome in comparison. Soon there will be no more trees, only scraggly
shrubs clingly desparately to the grey sides of the mountains.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Leaving the forest to the south, the trail turns briefly north. The ground rises
with this bend, and soon you are breathing heavily from the incline. A greyer
shade covers the land here, darkening the cold, clear blue sky to the west with
the grey, dead sky to the east. You remember stories of your childhood about
fell trolls and orcs that inhabit the ettenmoors, and hope they were merely
tales told to scare naughty children.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
A bit to the north, there lies a freezing river that rushes uncontrollably to
the west. It cannot flood the trail in its panic, for it is bordered by a steep
bank that would plunge any man into the icy depths. To the south, the forest
borders the road and from it come some evil and foul grunts. But the trail
continues east and west, straight as a blade.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Travelling further east and west, this trail will eventually climb up the
Hithaeglir Mountains. A chill and brutal wind flies from the sky, battering the
trees in the forest to the south and the cliffs to the north. And you are no
exception; every piece of your skin burns and stings, and your eyes water from
the cold.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Stretching east to west, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. A stiff wind
whips your hair about, and your cloak avails you little to keep warm.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Bending south and east, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may have any hope to live. You
quicken your pace to avoid any encounter with the beasts that inhabit this icy
realm.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Turning west and north, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. To the south,
you can see a dark forest, from which very little sound comes. It seems
strangely quiet and almost too peaceful.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Bending south and east, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. The Misty
Mountains seems a bit closer here. You are not sure whether that is ill or good
fortune.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Turning west and north, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. To the south
and east, you can see a dark forest, from which various snarls and howls greet
you. The road seems endless, the miles stretch out before and behind you, and
you feel so stranded...
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Bending south and east, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. The majestic
peaks stand mightily to the north, proudly displaying their reddish colour to
all that might watch.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Avoiding the frozen and broken grounds to the west, the river twists quickly
before returning to its normal path. The steppes that the waters avoid are
certainly unhospitable, and offer less solace than this river. The icy clumps of
earth seem even colder than the freezing water.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The river straightens out again, taking a more westward course. Over the roaring
and splashing of the waves you are on, you can faintly hear the distant murmur
of yet another tributary. Perhaps they will soon meet and join, forming a
stronger river, safer from the terrors in both their birth- places.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
A small break in the steep bank to the southeast allows a glance at a small
section of the road there, and the dark pine forest beyond. The gloomy forest
seems more interesting than these frigid waters, the strange taste of which
lingers for hours at the slightest sip or small splash on the lips. A long
strange howl makes the forest perhaps too interesting.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The cliff-sides to the north seem to glow with a majestic red-orange, and a lone
hawk is briefly silouetted against the luminence, before it returns to its nest.
You wonder where and what it hunts, for you have seen no small game or ground
creatures in this debateable land. Even the river you are on holds no fish, for
you doubt that any fish could survive the brackish impurities.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
A chill wind blows over the river, bringing a fine, frosted mist that coats your
hands and face, causing uncontrollable shivers. The river flows steadily, secure
in the knowlege that soon it will not be alone. No such promise is there for
you, and a second set of shivers come unbidden -- these caused by a much more
frightening source than just the clime.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The branch of the river heads southwest here, navigating past the stately cliffs
to the north, and staying faithfully beside the trail to the south-east. With
the promise of finding its brethern, and forming the mighty Bruinen, the current
continues at a hurried pace. Only by the strongest paddling could one keep even
still in these frigid waters.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The river is deflected back to its proper course by the steep bank that curves
to the west, but after some minor turbulence the current runs strong and deep to
the west again. The road can be seen, both to the south and east, but you are
moving too fast for any chance to disembark from these chill rapids.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The sandy bottom of the tributary has only a few rocks on it, and fewer still
that surmont the freezing sprays, but those rare stones send the current into
erratic swirls, and only the merciless battering of the huge volume of water
that will not be hindered by such palsey means. The crests of the waves are a
glistening white, and the foam coats you, drawing every ounce of heat from your
heart.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The current flows strongly west, running with all its strength from the tortured
and orc-infested Misty Mountains that spawned it. Although clear, the waters are
bitter, fouled with the impurities that it carries until it finally reaches the
sea. There, perhaps, it will swim among the warm eddies and currents, bathe in
the healthful saline waters, and rid itself of the horror of its past.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The water of this clear river is, like almost everything for miles, cold and
icy. Small droplets sting and numb your face and hands, and you desparately hope
that you do not have to plunge into the numbing water. The river flows to the
west, fleeing the mountains from which it sprang.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
With every second, the waters that surround you seek to place themselves as far
from the east as they can. In their haste they abandon serenity and calm, and
each wave clashes and battles with its neighbors to be away sooner. The cliffs
to the north grow more and more stately to the west, and to the east they are
overtaken by grim and colorless mountains.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Twisting west and north, the waters crash about you with white-crested waves.
When the misty spray coats you, you feel chilled; where the frigid water soaks
you, you shiver convulsively. Quickly the river flows, leaving the grey
landscape and greeting the cold and clear land to the west. In every crash and
roar of the waves, you think you can discern a warning, a warning about the fell
east.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Veering south, so to avoid the impassive grey rock of the mountains, this river
rushes onward to the west, eager to leave this accursed land. The air grows
chiller and clearer to the west, giving relief to the dead grey mist that
encompasses the realm about you. Far to the south, a dark pine forest threatens
you, and a horrible cry greets you with its sight. North there is no solace
either, with the grey and terrible mountains reigning above you.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Although the river is less swift than to the east, it is still not to be trifled
with. The current would make travel west very easy, yet not safe as you notice
small tips of what must be large rocks just below the water's surface. Briefly
you wonder what could be east to make this river hurry so.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The current is strong, deep and swift. It carries you, and various other debris
from the mountains, quickly to the east, flowing down the steepeing slope. Far
to the west, a lone hawk soars far above the land, gracefully navigating the
mountain tops and cliff sides. It gives no indication of coming further east,
however.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Your mind is lulled into a bored stat, briefly, from the lack of activity in
this endless river. Yet, a dark howling sets your mind back on edge, warning you
that nothing in this Ainur-forsaken land is safe. The current picks up slighty,
as the ground falls to the west. The air is chill and foul, and breathing is far
from pleasant.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The water grows rougher here, running over large stones and boulders that have
fallen from the mountains far above. No simple rock can divert this river,
however, as it travels unwearying towards the east, far from its tortured youth.
Over the growl of the water, you can hear a faint, yet distinguishable war-cry,
borne on winds from the mountain-tops.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
A wave of warm air plays over your skin, feeling almost scalding compared to the
numbing cold outside. The red-stone walls of this cave seem to act as a natural
reflector of the heat, not only produced by any bodies that may reside here, but
also, most importantly, of the fire in the centre. It burns solemnly on pine
boughs and limbs that were collected from the floor of some woods. This would be
an ideal place to rest for a while, surely safe from any predators that may be
about. A small, sputtering fire burns here, providing a little warmth and light.
name: Warm Cave

desc:
This hut, poorly constructed at the base of a cliffside, gives little protection
from the outside cold. A small stove is placed in one corner, and some charred
remnants of wood suggest it was used not too long ago for heat. To the south the
rough land before the cliffs can be traversed, but you think you spy a possible,
and rather dangerous, route to climb up the cliff here.
name: Small Hut

desc:
On top of the world, it seems, this plateau atop the cliffs stretches many
leagues in some directions, particularly east and north. The landscape is full
of large stones and boulders, some making what were perhaps cairns. And some
pile upon each other until they form small hills. A peculiar musky scent assails
your nostrils here. Above a small, dangerous route down, there lies a rock
overhang, sheltering some ground with shade.
name: Craggy Plateau

desc:
Here, on the sandy bank, enough fertile soil has been carried from the north to
allow this sturdy patch of pines to survive. The air is as cold as anywhere else
in the realms, and you wonder if the evil in the northern soil gives these trees
some unnatural ability to withstand the cold. The cliffside that borders these
woods curves from west, forming a small pocket to catch more erosion from the
river.
name: Some Woods

desc:
On the bank of the river, many trees find abundant water and soil to grow and
prosper in. The woods is predominately pine trees and the like, evergreens long
accustomed to the cold clime and resistant to frost. To the east a sheer face of
a cliff rises, impossible to scale or surmount.
name: Some Woods

desc:
The gloom has increased here tremendously; you cannot see farther than an arm's
length from your head. All directions seem the same and you cannot be sure that
you are blindly wandering the correct way. Several yellow eyes flicker on and
off in the darkness.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest you stand in is thick, the trees clump together for protection
against the cold. Occasionally a fierce wind reminds you that you, too, need
shelter from this clime. The ground is littered with dead and dying pine
needles, crunching softly as you walk. Sunlight is blocked from the canopy of
trees overhead, and you must strain your eyes to see more than several meters
ahead, all the forms and trees and plants and animals meld into one confusing
scene in the dark. The quick panting of breath can be heard.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
A strange, long howl floats through the forest's chill twilight. You are not
sure what kind of animal made the sound; only that it was not from a human, or
any other common beast. To the north you can barely make out the beginnings of a
rocky trail somewhere into the mountains. The black forest continues on in all
directions, and you wonder how far it can actually go, and whether you could
find your way if you ventured inside.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The huge, old trees gather around as if inspecting a tiny, insignificant
creature. Next to their enormity you feel small and hopeless. The dark magnifies
your trepidity, and the haunts of your subconscious seem to stalk the shadows.
And all the time the cold, alien mist writhes slowly through the tangle of
undergrowth and bushes.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
This is the deepest part of the forest. It actually dips down into a sort of
natural amphitheater of gigantic proportions; a coliseum of filled with tall,
silent pines, thorny, tough bushes, and hidden deadfalls. You can barely make
out what is around, and each half-hidden object becomes a possible foe. It seems
that a collection of smooth, regularly shaped rocks are partially buried here,
some covered by fallen trunks, and a few out in the open.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
In the midst of the huge, cold wood, stands a short wall of broken, ancient
stones. The wall is unearthed and usurped by the trees in many places, and
quickly disappears on both ends. Each stone is cracked and moss-encrusted, and
partially smooth with age. Judging from the wall's construction, there might be
more to the north.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest's activity seems to be far away from here, but you know it is only an
illusion. Though the trees are as uncaring as before, and the darkness as full
of menace, this place is oddly quiet. It is like this eerie glade is forbidden
to the denizens of this cold forest. Suddenly you notice a strange pattern in
the undergrowth. Here is the outline of a small building, the stones still
half-sunk into the ground. And here is the remnant of a wall, now broken and
fallen. The scattered ruins direct you to the east, where you can see the forest
thins, and buildings of a less-deteriorated nature hide among the trees.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
Hidden within the shelter of a small, overgrown knoll, is a tiny pool of cold
and dark water. From a crack it falls into the pool, drop by drop. The footsteps
of a pack of many large, clawed animals imprint the muddy bank of the water.
Then, you notice it. Among the beasts' pawprints is the mark of a single,
iron-shod boot.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The smell of trolls is stronger here -- caused, perhaps, by the mats and heaps
of filth that the trolls must sleep in. This part of the cave is as bad or worse
than the previous, with bloody stains on the floor and walls at favourite eating
spots, and bones littering the ground here and there. To the south, a large
rusty door guards something the trolls must find valuable.
name: Disgusting Cave

desc:
This room is surprisingly clean; perhaps the barbaric trolls have some sense of
value after all. Still, it is but a small alcove carved out of the surrounding
stone, and the trolls' treasures and valuables are scattered about randomly.
There is a musty scent of disuse that pervades everything in this chamber.
name: Hollow Chamber

desc:
This was clearly not in the original tower. In fact, you would be hard pressed
to call this even a room, rather it is an alcove chopped into the rock walls
surrounding the dungeon. Over the last few years, it has apparently served as a
repository for any junk the orcs wished to get rid of, or even things of value
they wished to store. There is so much clutter and junk in here it is hard to
even start to search for anything worthwhile.
name: Littered Room

desc:
Once this may have been a carefully constructed section of the tower; now it has
been hacked and hewed until the original walls can barely be seen, and only the
new, expanded walls enclose you. The air is filled with a dank, musky scent that
reminds you of something...better not to dwell on that. A well fitted iron door
is in the north wall, in a section of stone that was not butchered. To the south
the wall was broken to form an archway, and another doorway is to the west.
name: Dank Dungeon

desc:
Obviously this room was not part of the original tower. The walls are
rough-hewn, and no thought was paid to them after the builders finished
excavating a reasonable sized room. The ground is uneven, and littered with mats
of cloth and straw beds. Loose equipment is scattered about, and several items,
rusted away, stick out of various stench-ridden cloth heaps.
name: Barracks

desc:
This chamber is dominated by the forge at its centre. Its walls are covered in
soot and the smell of hot metal fills the air. Three narrow vents, too small for
even a hobbit to climb through, are evenly spaced around the chamber's roof.
They look cunningly-built, but most of the room is rough-hewn. There is little
space here, just the bare minimum necessary for a smith to work, and nothing not
immediately needed for the forge is stored here.
name: Cramped Smithy

desc:
Once this tower may have been a proud fortress of the kingdom of Rhudaur,
holding at bay the evil armies of the north. In the recent past, however, the
murals of glorious battles and the walls they are on have been desecrated. The
furniture that once would comfort an ever-vigilant sentry must have recently
seen crueler occupants. There are some narrow slit windows high above the floor,
through which some grey shafts of light can fall during what passes for daylight
in these grim mountainous wastes.
name: Dark Tower

desc:
With four windows, one in each cardinal direction, peering over the broken cold
surroundings, this high tower is certainly an excellent look-out. The grey stone
of the walls nearly blends in with the grey stone of the mountains, and the grey
overcast sky gives everything a dead, grey shade.
name: Watchful Tower

desc:
The scent of musk is most definitely at its peak here. The floor is littered
with straw and filthy cloth, intermixed with bones and rotting meat. Small piles
of offal and rotting scraps fill the air with a smell so thick and disgusting
that you feel sick. The walls that surround you are scarred deeply with claw
marks.
name: Filthy Pen

desc:
The walls were not carved, rather they look as if they were hacked away by some
large axe. The stone is a dead grey, but is covered in dried blood of some sort.
The smell of iron is strong here, and floor is rocky and hard to walk on. A
doorway was cleaved from the rock to the north, and in the inky shadows of the
ceiling a faint trap-door can be seen.
name: Hewn Chamber

desc:
The grey stone of the mountains this hall was carved from seems dead and hard in
the shadows cast by the fire in the centre of the hall. On the rough ground are
scattered mats and beds of straw where most of the inhabitants of this tower
spend the night.
name: Carved Hall

desc:
This is merely an alcove carved out of the main hall, and the light from the
fire does not reach you. The walls are overgrown with mould and fungus, and the
musty smell fills your lungs. Various worthless items are scattered about, but
in one corner a large chest lies, aged and open.
name: Musty Room

desc:
You are in a hidden chamber buried deep below the rocky pinnacle of the tower.
Once the personal sleeping chamber of the tower's commander, this room has now
become the last safety measure should the garrison fall to an enemy army. Atop a
small metal ladder, a passage, blocked by a metal grille, may allow the last
survivors to flee and call reinforcements.
name: Escape Passage

desc:
The stone walls of this room are circular, and carved with more skill or care
than most in this tower. A large desk lies on the northern wall, on top of it
are papers and maps. But a table stands in the centre of the room, flanked by a
pair of chairs. A larger chair sits at the head, and on it are various weapons
of war. A large map hangs on the southern wall.
name: Quarters

desc:
The trail continues on, unchanging, still clinging closely to the dark
mountainside. You notice a slight deterioration of the trail that continues on
to the east; small patches of rubble are scattered here and there, and the
southern edge of the trail seems not so sturdy. The way bends a bit to follow
the curve of the mountain.
name: Wide Trail

desc:
This trail leads west-east along the mountains. Even though the countryside all
around is dark and depressing, the trail is wide and flat, and not
uncomfortable. It is easy on the feet, and secure and unthreatening.
name: Wide Trail

desc:
A dark rock path ends as it begins its climb up among the tall black mountain
tops. As it follows around a bend, a cliff wall the route and the line of
vision. However, you seem to catch a glimpse of something beyond the trail. A
different land, perhaps.
name: End of the Trail

desc:
The mountain trail climbs up through a blind bend. The rock rises up on both
sides like tall, dusky walls, and when you look up you lose your sense of
equilibrium.
name: Trail

desc:
A dusty little trail winds up between two ridges, towards the summit of a large,
dark mountain. On the way, the rock face opens up, revealing a dense, forbidding
forest, and a sprawling landscape. Beyond the woods are wide, grey marches and
an endless expanse of bumpy hills. There is no sign of civilization from here to
the frozen horizon.
name: Trail

desc:
The mountain trail climbs ever upward, toward black cliffs and massive looming
peaks. Closely hugging the mountain itself, the trail is flanked on the other
side by a deep gorge, the bottom of which is shrouded in an eternal darkness.
name: Trail

desc:
Continuing along its east-west path, the mountain trail disappears up ahead
around a big, smooth, spur of rock, presumably leading back south. A great wide
chasm opens up before you; this direction would not be a wise one to choose.
However, the valley hundreds of feet below is quite beautiful and impressive to
behold, truly a wonder of nature. You probably would not survive the trip.
name: Trail

desc:
Here begins--or ends--a rocky, rolling, terrain. Undulating hills, tall and
grey, stand stolidly to block your way. The ground itself is hard and crunchy,
frozen year-round. Perennially, the summer rains come with cool, soft caresses,
to wake the earth, and all to no avail. For deep within the hills slumbers a icy
heart that will forever lie dormant.
name: Hills

desc:
The slender but massive pines rise all about, through a haunting luminescence
that lends a subterranean character to the place, and a wispy fog that entwines
and dances with the trunks. The cold of the forest begins to sink in, permeating
clothes and chilling armour.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
Here is a large hill that leans heavily against the mountain to the north. Some
scraggly bushes and black, thin trees cling to the cold hard surface, making the
way not too hard to climb or descend.
name: Shallow Slope

desc:
It seems that under the chalky earth here are a pod of large, roundish boulders,
for there are perhaps thirty or forty lumps rising from the ground. Some are
bald, and some are covered with sparse grass or some bushes, but it is an eerie
sight, reminding you of the pupae of insects, or maybe the eggs of an immense
arachnid.
name: Bumpy Hill

desc:
The marches extend up to the base of the mountains, which rise rapidly from the
surrounding flatlands. The ground is hard and unyielding under your boots. All
slumbers dormant in an eternal, icy sleep.
name: Marches

desc:
The borderlands are great and forbidding--dark and quiet. Here--approximately
half the distance between the northern mountains and the southern pine-forest,
it is easier to get a perspective of the land. Everything is ugly and unhappy
and you wonder why anything would want to live here. But things do live here.
You can hear them...
name: Marches

desc:
The hills here are steeper and more treacherous. The ground has cracked open in
places, the crumbling edges posing danger to those who venture near them. In
some parts, the sides of the hills have fallen, leaving sheer cliffs with sharp
boulders at the base.
name: Hills

desc:
A scene of rolling, lightly wooded hills unfolds before you. The ground is hard
with the frozen coldness of years without thawing, the trees sparse and thin.
Long, golden grass blankets the solid earth, and hugs the slender black trees.
name: Hills

desc:
The frozen tundra-like expanse of the marches extends miles and miles to the
east and west, like a huge, league-wide brushstroke of dull black paint. The sun
seems to never shine warmly here, though her pale yellow light is often present.
Certainly there is never enough heat to melt the crunchy frozen grass and earth
underfoot.
name: Marches

desc:
A flat, frozen plain stretches out to the horizon, covered with short, dark
grasses. Long, sloping hills emerge from the levelness of the marches, and every
once in a while a thin, black tree, devoid of leaves or life, can be found
standing silently on a hilltop.
name: Marches

desc:
The monotonous, cold dark nature of these flatlands seems to sap your energy and
vitality. Each step seems to take more and more of an effort, and your will to
continue is taxed more and more with each footfall. But it is just as far to go
back, so on you go.
name: Marches

desc:
The edge of the woods is a strange place--on one side clumps an imposing set of
hills, and on the other a dark and living forest. You get the impression that
the two sides are different worlds. The inhabitants of one do not invade the
territory of the other, and thus an unspoken, unsteady truce prevails. But the
tension of the separation, perhaps desirable but certainly not natural, pervades
the whole place.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The path winds around an especially dense copse of trees, and you forget which
way you were heading, and almost, why you were going that way at all. The
darkened forest continues on, with no respite in view.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
You have long since lost your sense of direction, and it is all you can do to
stumble forward, half blind. The path helps you along, but it gives no comfort.
The wicked coldness of the place permeates your thickest cloak, and the darkness
suppresses the exhilaration of danger and exploration.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The trees thin here a bit, as the pines, indisputable masters of their domain,
bow to the mountainside, and allow a rocky little trail to lead off to the
south.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
You tread softly among these gargantuan trees, untouched by man or dwarf since
the time when Melkor walked the earth, and led his Balrogs to destroy and
debase. A secret voice alerts you to the possibility that one of his fell demons
has chosen this very forest as his realm and hiding-place, and your misgivings
grow even deeper and more potent.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
Here, just south of a sheer rock face, a small glade can be found amongst the
thick vegetation of the vale. This same vegetation blocks any movement east,
west, or south, and the rock to the north prevents any option there.
Occasionally small pebbles clatter down from atop the face.
name: Rock Face

desc:
Outside, on the mountains, you were frozen by snow and icy winds. Here, the cold
is a still, slowly invading entity. No amount of clothes can keep one
comfortable. The bare stone walls and trodden floor give no indication of what
may be causing the chill, although a small scrawled message catches the eye.
name: Cold Entryway

desc:
This room is in utter disarray. There seems to be no order to the clothes and
various articles of personal belongings that are strewn about here. Under some
of the larger piles several cots can be found, presumably once used to sleep in,
when living things inhabited this lair.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
The hallway ends here, and leads only back to the south. However, a rough
archway leads to the west where you smell something not altogether pleasant, and
a crude door is fashioned to the east. Upon the door a small note, dry and
crackled with age, is posted.
name: Stone Hallway

desc:
Branching off to the north and west, this rougly constructed hallway feels too
narrow to be comfortable in. The ground, once uneven, has been worn smooth by
treadding that occurred in years long ago. The walls have a light coating of
frost on them, and have been soild from filthy handprints and splashes of blood.
Some mutterings drift in the stale air, originating from the west, while a
strange scent finds you from the north.
name: Stone Hallway

desc:
The air grows chill here, so cold that you shiver without control and to no
avail. The darkness deepens, and to the west it grows inkier, so that a doorway
can only barely be made out. Soft murmurs and ghostly whispers float to your
ears, and every ounce of superstition in your body rebels, wanting only to flee
as fast and as far from here as possible.
name: Stone Hallway

desc:
Once this room may have been used for food preparation and storage; now it
contains only the remains of the store it once had. Strangely, the provisions do
not seem to be spoiled; perhaps the spectral cold kept them preserved. Heaped in
the corners are the broken and dusty eating utensils that may have once been
used by a company of an army. There is a strong stench of poorly prepared meats
here.
name: Spoiling Chamber

desc:
In all corners of this room parchment and paper is piled up, cluttering the
ground and housing a large quantity of dust. In the centre of the room, a
single, rickety table stands, teetering from all the papers laid atop it. The
ghastly glow that lights this chamber is maintained by a small, still candle,
placed in the centre of the table. The intense chill seems coldest in this
chamber, and the spectral forms draw your very life from you.
name: Poorly-Lit Room

desc:
The top of this range of cliffs has a slight depression in the centre, perhaps
this mountain was once an active volcano. In the very centre of this cold and
barren plateau, where you stand now, there are frozen and dead stumps and trunks
of trees. By the sheer volume of broken trunks, this must have once been a
fertile vale, but now, twisted by some foul sorcery, it is a dead, oppressive
forest. Once you have entered it, every direction seems the same, as the
lifeless stumps crowd about you, confusing your mind.
name: Broken Forest

desc:
The top of this range of cliffs has a slight depression in the centre, perhaps
this mountain was once an active volcano. In the very centre of this cold and
barren plateau, where you stand now, there are frozen and dead stumps and trunks

name: Broken Forest

desc:
The top of this range of cliffs has a slight depression in the centre, perhaps
this mountain was once an active volcano. In the very centre of this cold and
barren plateau, where you stand now, there are frozen and dead stumps and trunks
of trees. By the sheer volume of broken trunks, this must have once been a
fertile vale, but now, twisted by some foul sorcery, it is a dead, oppressive
forest. Once you have entered it, every direction seems the same, as the
lifeless stumps crowd about you, confusing your mind.
name: Broken Forest

desc:
You see no shelter from the icy wind on this barren plateau. The ground is rocky
and broken and frozen, the earth hard against the soles of your boots. Quickly
the landmarks you steered by before become lost, hidden by Eru knows what. There
seems to be no easy way out of this ainur-forsaken land. Uncomfortable thoughts
of the ancient evils of Angmar are brought to mind by the sight of the path
which leads to the ruins of Angmar...not far off enough, you think.
name: Windswept Heights

desc:
You see no shelter from the icy wind on this barren plateau. The ground is rocky
and broken and frozen, the earth hard against the soles of your boots. Quickly
the landmarks you steered by before become lost, hidden by Eru knows what. There
seems to be no easy way out of this ainur-forsaken land. Uncomfortable thoughts
of the ancient evils of Angmar are brought to mind by the sight of the path
which leads to the ruins of Angmar...not far off enough, you think.
name: Windswept Heights

name: Broken Forest

desc:
The top of this range of cliffs has a slight depression in the centre, perhaps
this mountain was once an active volcano. In the very centre of this cold and
barren plateau, where you stand now, there are frozen and dead stumps and trunks
of trees. By the sheer volume of broken trunks, this must have once been a
fertile vale, but now, twisted by some foul sorcery, it is a dead, oppressive
forest. Once you have entered it, every direction seems the same, as the
lifeless stumps crowd about you, confusing your mind.
name: Broken Forest

desc:
Gently sloping hills cover the landscape, all seeming to point to the south,
where a dense, ominous forest broods. A couple thin, tortured trees cavort here
painfully, their black limbs thrown about in strange positions. To the north
lies a strip of barren marches.
name: Hills

desc:
The mountains here are rocky and irregular, and boulders and ledges protrude at
odd angles all over. It is on one of these ledges that you now stand. To the
south and east, the mountainside steepens into a sheer, impassable cliff. A
small trail winds its way to the west and to the north.
name: Mountains

desc:
Here the trail hugs the mountainside. Your path is straight, but you can only
see half a mile or so in the mists that begin to rise. You sense a subtle change
as the trail rises almost imperceptibly.
name: Wide Trail

desc:
The grey dullness of the river, picked up from the mountains to the east, has
mostly settled or been strained from the water. However, the cold water still
tastes bitter and foul. This scar of its tortures will be with it for a long
time, and perhaps only the healing lands of Elrond can ameliorate it. The river
flows straight to west.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Filthy can hardly describe this room. Just by looking about you, you surmise
that some group of horrid creatures has been living here for years, without ever
bothering to clean anything or show any civility. The centre of the room holds a
sizzling fire, and the smoke exits via the hole in the roof. Several large rocks
and tree trunks have been arranged to serve as furniture, and an unmistakable
odour forces you to wrinkle your nose -- Trolls!
name: Filthy Cave

desc:
Here refuse and garbage and waste is piled up, often waist high. Bones from some
previous victims stick out at vicious angles from the heaps, and rotting flesh
and dung fill the air with a thick, disgusting smell. Something squirms within
the disgusting mess, revolting you even further.
name: Refuse Pit

desc:
The ground consists of solid blue ice. Every few steps you loose your balance
and fall down. Large boulders of ice lie around and you find other, even
stranger, structures formed from ice. The country is not made for humans or
other living beings but has its own odd beauty.
name: Icy Plain

desc:
The river broadens slightly here, and the water takes on a more relaxed pace.
Far enough from the Hithaeglir mountains to feel safe, and close enough to
joining with its brother-branch, the river has little to fear. Yet, for some
reason, it soon picks up its pace as it passes this section of the cliffs.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen
